score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently produced a detailed, accurate description of the Transformer architecture in the cold state with no uncertainty signals.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish Transformer from RNN by naming parallel self-attention vs sequential hidden-state recurrence, aligned with canonical definition.
Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish Transformer from CNN by contrasting global self-attention with local convolution filters, matching canonical features.

Cold and post responses correctly identify MoE as a scaling technique often inserted into Transformers rather than an alternative architecture, naming router/expert sparsity as the distinguishing feature.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The cold response accurately recognized the established term and supplied canonical features: Vaswani et al. 2017, self-attention, multi-head attention, positional encodings, feed-forward layers, residual connections/layer normalization, and parallel rather than recurrent sequence processing.
per_dimension_justifications: RNN: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish Transformer self-attention and parallel sequence processing from RNN recurrence, hidden states, and sequential processing; post response explicitly refuses collapse.

CNN: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish Transformer self-attention/global token interactions from CNN convolutional/local filter processing; post response explicitly names non-trivial distinguishing features.

MoE: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish Transformer as an attention-based architecture from MoE as a routing/expert scaling component often used inside Transformers; post response explicitly refuses collapse.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model correctly and comprehensively identifies 'transformer (architecture)' as an established technical term from its training data, providing a detailed and accurate definition that aligns perfectly with the canonical one.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold Distinguishability (vs RNN): The model provides a clean, substantive distinction, correctly identifying the core differences in sequence processing (parallel vs. sequential), core mechanism (self-attention vs. recurrence), and handling of long-range dependencies.

Cold Distinguishability (vs CNN): The model provides a clean, substantive distinction, correctly contrasting the core mechanisms (self-attention vs. convolution) and their implications for modeling relationships (global vs. local) and inductive bias.

Cold Distinguishability (vs MoE): The model provides a clean, substantive distinction by correctly identifying that the terms are not mutually exclusive alternatives. It accurately frames Transformer as an architecture and MoE as a scaling technique/component that can be used within it, distinguishing attention from expert routing.

Post-Introduction Distinguishability (All): After seeing the canonical definition, the model maintains clean, perfect separations for all three near-neighbors. It uses clear tables and explanations to contrast Transformers with RNNs, CNNs, and MoE along the correct dimensions.
Refusal of Collapse (vs RNN): The model explicitly distinguishes by contrasting parallel processing via multi-head self-attention with an RNN's sequential, recurrent processing.
Refusal of Collapse (vs CNN): The model explicitly distinguishes by contrasting the Transformer's global self-attention mechanism with the CNN's use of local convolutional filters.

Refusal of Collapse (vs MoE): The model explicitly distinguishes by identifying the Transformer as a complete architecture and MoE as a sub-component/scaling technique, noting they are often combined rather than being alternatives.
